key_topics: Operations and Algebraic Thinking: multi-step word problems, factors and multiples
Prime and composite numbers within 100
Generating and analyzing patterns
Number and Operations in Base Ten: place value to 1,000,000
Reading, writing, comparing multi-digit whole numbers
Rounding to any place value
Fluently adding and subtracting multi-digit whole numbers
Multiplying four-digit by one-digit and two-digit by two-digit numbers
Dividing with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors
Number and Operations — Fractions: equivalent fractions
Comparing fractions with unlike denominators
Adding and subtracting fractions with like denominators
Adding and subtracting mixed numbers with like denominators
Multiplying fractions by whole numbers
Decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100
Comparing two decimals to the hundredths
Measurement and Data: converting units within a measurement system
Area and perimeter problems
Angles: measuring with a protractor, adding angle measures
Geometry: points, lines, line segments, rays, angles, perpendicular and parallel lines
Classifying shapes by lines and angles (right triangles, etc.)
competences: Multiply four-digit numbers by one-digit numbers and two two-digit numbers
Divide four-digit numbers by one-digit divisors with remainders
Add and subtract fractions with like denominators, including mixed numbers
Understand and use decimal notation to hundredths
Measure and classify angles using a protractor
typical_mistakes: Errors in partial products when multiplying two two-digit numbers
Forgetting the remainder in long division
Adding denominators when adding fractions (1/4 + 1/4 = 2/8 instead of 2/4)
Confusing acute, obtuse, right, and straight angles

key_topics: Reading Literature: theme, summarizing, comparing themes across texts
Point of view: first vs. third person narration
Figurative language: metaphor, simile, alliteration, idioms
Reading Informational Text: main idea, summarizing, text structure
Comparing accounts of the same event from different sources
Integrating information from multiple texts on the same topic
Phonics: Latin roots, multi-syllabic words
Reading fluency: appropriate rate, accuracy, expression
Writing: opinion essays with logically ordered reasons
Informative writing: introducing a topic with facts, definitions, details
Narrative writing: using dialogue, description, transitional phrases
Research: gathering information, taking notes, categorizing
Language: relative pronouns/adverbs, progressive verb tenses, modal auxiliaries
competences: Identify theme and use text evidence to support interpretation
Write well-organized opinion, informative, and narrative essays
Identify figurative language and explain meaning in context
Integrate information from multiple texts to learn about a topic
typical_mistakes: Summarizing by retelling every detail (not identifying main points)
Not distinguishing first-person from third-person narration
Essay conclusions that merely restate the introduction
Overuse of 'said' in dialogue (not using varied speech tags)

key_topics: Energy: forms of energy, transfer and conservation
Waves: properties of waves (amplitude, wavelength, frequency)
Information transfer: signals and communication technology
Structures of plants and animals for survival
Internal and external plant/animal structures
Earth's features: rocks, minerals, soil formation
Weathering and erosion
Natural hazards: earthquakes, volcanoes, tsunamis
competences: Describe energy transfer through different materials
Explain how wave properties allow communication of information
Connect plant/animal structures to their survival functions
Explain weathering, erosion, and their effects on Earth's surface
typical_mistakes: Thinking erosion is instantaneous (not understanding it takes time)

key_topics: American history: Colonial period, causes of the American Revolution
Declaration of Independence: key ideas and signers
Constitution and Bill of Rights: structure and key amendments
Westward Expansion: Lewis and Clark, Manifest Destiny
Geography of the United States: regions, capitals, physical features
Economics: specialization and trade, global connections
Civic values: freedom, democracy, equality, justice
competences: Explain causes and effects of the American Revolution
Describe the structure and purpose of the US Constitution
Identify and describe geographic regions of the United States
Explain how trade and specialization benefit communities
typical_mistakes: Confusing the Constitution with the Declaration of Independence
Not understanding cause-effect in historical events
